texto: I saw a painting once where the artist had actually done that--signed his work in blood. ... When I saw that, I thought it was as if the man who had painted the picture wanted to say to me, Well, you did ask what this actually cost.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Neil Bartlett: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a autoestima. O trecho “I saw a painting once where the artist had actually done that--signed his work in blood. ... When…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que autoestima, art, blood pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de trabalho e blood.

texto: Imagine your body becoming that of a stranger. Imagine the sensation of it being not yours, as you discover what it feels like to do this, or to have this happen to you, for the very first time. Imagine it happening with sickening slowness, or with shocking speed, that discovery. And then imagine knowing it has come too late.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Neil Bartlett: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Imagine your body becoming that of a stranger. Imagine the sensation of it being not yours, as yo…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sucesso, dismay, firsts pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de firsts e self-discovery.

frase_original: Imagine your body becoming that of a stranger. Imagine the sensation of it being not yours, as you discover what it feels like to do this, or to have this happen to you, for the very first time. Imagine it happening with sickening slowness, or with shocking speed, that discovery. And then imagine knowing it has come too late.

texto: Look at him, lying there. Why should he need me to give him strength--to watch over him, and always be worrying how he's feeling? Surely he'll find it himself. Isn't that what we believe, that we do always somehow find the strength? That the path will lead out of the forest; that the riddle will be solved; that the child never dies.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Neil Bartlett que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “Look at him, lying there. Why should he need me to give him strength--to watch over him, and alwa…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre familia, belief, fairytales, com foco em fairytales e weakness-and-strength.
